subject: Ditch on Harvard Ave completely clogged with leaves and debris
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the roadside ditch along Harvard Ave between 25th and 28th has been clogged solid for at least three weeks. After Tuesday's rain we had standing water stretching halfway across the parking lane — I couldn't open my car door without stepping into ankle-deep water. The ditch looks like it hasn't been cleared since spring. Is there a scheduled maintenance crew for this? I'd love to know when someone can get out here.
address_hint: Harvard Ave between 25th Ave and 28th Ave

subject: Ditch on Harvard Ave completely clogged with leaves and debris
body_clean: Ms. Marchetti — got your message. We had two crews out on the east side ditch runs last week but Harvard Ave south of 26th missed the pass due to a breakdown on Truck 4. Scheduling a return visit for Thursday or Friday of this week depending on weather. Once we clear the leaf mat we'll rod the inlet at the 27th Ave crossing — that's likely where it's backing up. Should drain properly within 24 hours of service. I appreciate you being specific about the location.
address_hint: Harvard Ave between 25th Ave and 28th Ave

subject: Sewage smell coming from storm drain on Morris Ave
body_clean: Good morning Mayor. I live at 2108 Morris Ave and for the past week or so there's been a strong sewage-type odor coming out of the storm drain in front of my property. It's not every hour — seems worse in the mornings and after it rains. I do not have any sewer issues inside my home. This is definitely from the street drain. My elderly mother visits us and the smell is genuinely unpleasant and alarming. Could someone check whether there's a cross-connection or blockage causing this?

subject: Sewage smell coming from storm drain on Morris Ave
body_clean: Mr. Wojtyla, I sent a crew to camera the storm main on Morris Ave this morning. What they found was a significant grease and organic debris accumulation about 40 feet downstream of your inlet — not a cross-connection, but it's generating hydrogen sulfide as it breaks down, which is the rotten-egg smell you're noticing. We're scheduling a jetter flush for next Tuesday. That should eliminate the odor within a couple of days after service. If it persists more than a week after the flush, please call Public Works directly at 708-555-0142 and we'll do a second pass.

subject: Alley behind Rice Ave flooding every time it rains
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, the alley behind the 2300 block of Rice Ave turns into a river every time we get more than half an inch of rain. Last Thursday it came all the way up to the bottom of my back gate and I was terrified it would get into my basement. I've lived here six years and it's getting worse every summer. There's a catch basin back there that I think is broken — the grate sits lower than the surrounding alley surface. Can Public Works look at this before the next storm system comes through?

subject: Alley behind Rice Ave flooding every time it rains
body_clean: Ms. Simmons — I pulled the maintenance record on that alley catch basin. Last inspection was 2021; it's overdue. We went out this afternoon and confirmed the frame has settled about two inches below grade and the sump is packed with sand and gravel. We'll raise the frame and clean the sump out next week, Tuesday being the target. I can't guarantee it solves 100% of the flooding — that alley has a systemic low point — but getting that basin functional again should give the water somewhere to go quickly instead of spreading. I'll note it for the capital drainage list as well.

subject: Collapsed culvert on Granville Ave near 19th
body_clean: Mayor, there is what appears to be a collapsed culvert on Granville Ave just east of 19th Ave. The road surface has a visible depression over it — about four feet across — and you can hear the pipe crunch if you drive over slowly. I'm worried about a sinkhole developing. Two of my neighbors have been going around it. This looks serious and I think it needs attention before someone's car drops into it or worse. I tried calling the public works line twice but just got voicemail.

subject: Collapsed culvert on Granville Ave near 19th
body_clean: Mr. Looney — thank you for the call-out. We sent a crew this morning and they confirmed a 24-inch corrugated metal culvert has partially collapsed under the pavement. We've placed barricades and a temporary patch over the depression as of noon today. The full repair requires saw-cutting the asphalt, removing the failed pipe, and installing HDPE replacement — we're looking at a 3–4 day job once materials are on site. Flagging Verdi Excavating to mobilize by end of next week. Road stays reduced to one lane with barricades until then. Apologies for the voicemail miss.

subject: Collapsed culvert on Granville Ave near 19th
body_clean: That's a relief — I drove by around 2pm and saw the barricades already up. Thank you for moving quickly. I'll keep my kids off that stretch until you're done. Really glad someone took this seriously.

subject: Standing water in alley off 50th Ave won't go away
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo. I'm reaching out about the alley that runs behind the homes on 50th Ave between Washington Blvd and Madison St. There's been a large puddle — really more of a pond — sitting in the middle of the alley for nearly two weeks. It does not drain at all. Mosquitoes are absolutely terrible back there and my kids can't play near the garage without getting eaten alive. I believe there may be a blocked or broken drain inlet. Can someone from the village investigate?
address_hint: 50th Ave alley between Washington Blvd and Madison St

subject: Ditch on Englewood Ave keeps overflowing onto sidewalk
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — I've been meaning to write for a while. The roadside ditch on Englewood Ave between Bellwood Ave and Frederick Ave overflows onto the sidewalk every time it rains hard. The water freezes once temps drop, making the walk to the bus stop genuinely dangerous. I slipped there last November and tweaked my knee pretty good. I know winter is coming soon and I really don't want to see that situation repeat. Is there any way to get that ditch cleared out before it freezes over?
address_hint: Englewood Ave between Bellwood Ave and Frederick Ave

subject: Foul smell from drain near Hirsch and 25th
body_clean: Good afternoon. I live on Hirsch Ave and every morning when I walk my dog toward 25th Ave there's an awful smell — like rotten eggs — coming from the storm drain on the northeast corner. It's been consistent for about three weeks. My neighbor smells it too. It seems worse after temperatures go above freezing during the day and then drop again at night. I don't know if that matters. Is this something the village looks into, or is it a county issue?

subject: Foul smell from drain near Hirsch and 25th
body_clean: Ms. Grabowski, thank you for writing and for the detail about the temperature pattern — that's genuinely useful. This is a village matter and I'm copying Frank DiMeo at Public Works so his team can schedule a camera inspection of that drain and the lateral leading to it. Organic matter that freezes and thaws repeatedly can generate exactly the odor you're describing. We'll get someone out within a few business days and follow up with you directly.

subject: Alley flooding behind Monroe St is damaging my garage
body_clean: Mayor, I own the property at 1742 Monroe St and the alley behind it floods every spring. This year was the worst — water came under the garage door and damaged drywall on two walls and some stored belongings. I've been dealing with this for four years and submitted one prior request to Public Works with no lasting fix. The water appears to back up from the north end of the alley. At this point I'm wondering if the village bears any responsibility for the damage given the lack of action. I'd like to understand what the plan is going forward.

subject: Small sinkhole forming next to culvert on St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Hi there. I noticed a small but growing depression in the ground on St. Paul Ave right next to the culvert that goes under the driveway apron into the ditch around 3100. It's maybe 8 inches across right now but it appeared last week after the heavy rain and looks bigger than it did Monday. I don't know if this means the culvert is failing underneath or if it's just soil washout. I parked cones around it so nobody steps in it. Wanted to let someone know before it becomes a bigger problem.

subject: Drainage ditch on Eastern Ave blocked by landscaping debris
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — the ditch on Eastern Ave near 320 is completely blocked by mulch and grass clippings that someone dumped there. Probably a landscaping crew working on one of the commercial lots. Water from Tuesday's rain couldn't get through and it backed up all the way to the curb. I took pictures if that helps. This happens every few months on the same stretch. Is there an ordinance against dumping yard waste in the ditch? And can someone clear it out before the next rain?

subject: Ice dam forming from blocked ditch on Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Hello Mayor. I'm at 2019 Bellwood Ave and there's a serious ice hazard building up on the street in front of my house. The roadside ditch is completely clogged — leaves from fall never got cleaned out — and meltwater from last week's snow has nowhere to go so it's running across the road and freezing solid. We've had two cars slide on it. I salted in front of my property but the water just keeps coming. This really needs attention before someone gets hurt.

subject: Alley behind 19th Ave near Madison flooding my yard
body_clean: Good morning Mayor. Every spring when we get a good soaking rain the alley behind 19th Ave near Madison St floods and the water comes through the fence into my backyard. I've had to re-seed the grass three times in four years. I believe the alley doesn't drain properly because it crests in the wrong place — there's a bump right in the middle that sends water toward both ends of the alley but the south end has no outlet. I'm not an engineer but this seems like a grading issue. Is this something Public Works can evaluate?
